delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

summary: Leslie’s EPIC Writing course focuses on two key writing genres: academic writing and journalism. Students will first learn the fundamentals of structured academic writing, including formal sentence construction, logical paragraph development, and essay writing. The second half of the course is dedicated to journalism through the Life in Kochi project, where students will act as citizen journalists, researching and writing articles for a global readership. By engaging in real writing with a real audience, students will enhance their communication skills, develop critical thinking, and gain a deeper connection to their local community.
aims: This course aims to develop students' English writing skills by engaging them in real-world writing experiences. Through academic writing and journalism, students will refine their ability to write clearly, logically, and effectively for a global audience.
goals: Develop clear, structured academic writing skills, including sentence construction, paragraph organization, and essay composition.
Research, interview, and write journalistic articles using the “four-box feature” structure.
Engage with local news and events to create relevant and meaningful content for a global audience.
Revise and refine writing based on feedback to produce high-quality, publishable work.
Foster Active Citizenship for Effective Democratic Participation
textbooks: All materials developed by the instructor.
prereq: All EPIC students must pass both a written exam and an oral interview conducted by the EPIC teachers.
prep: This course will require students to schedule and conduct interviews in the community on their own time. This may involve some travel, which students will need to arrange themselves. In addition, the drafting process is labor intensive, and students must do most of the writing outside of class time, as much of the class time will be devoted to lectures and discussions about the ethics, procedures, and aims of student journalism.

officeHour.name: David Leslie
officeHour.place: In class or online by schedule
